questions.text: Why does Allah swear by time in this Surah?
questions.answer: Because time is the capital of life: it is always decreasing and witnesses how people use it.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that personal faith alone is enough even if you ignore others.
questions.answer: False. It includes mutual advising to truth and patience.

questions.text: What does it mean that mankind is in loss?
questions.answer: Without the four conditions, people waste their lives and lose their Hereafter.

questions.text: Why are faith and righteous deeds mentioned together?
questions.answer: Faith must produce action, and action without faith lacks the right foundation.

questions.text: What does mutual advising to truth and patience look like in practice?
questions.answer: Truth: Encouraging each other to honesty, tawhid, justice, and sticking to the Quran and Sunnah. Patience: Supporting each other through trials, staying consistent in worship, and not giving up when doing right is hard.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that time management is only a worldly productivity topic.
questions.answer: False. It makes time a spiritual issue tied to salvation.

questions.text: What is one modern example of ‘loss’ according to this Surah’s logic?
questions.answer: Spending years in distraction—scrolling, vanity, or sin—without faith, deeds, truth, and patience.

questions.text: How can a believer protect their time from being wasted?
questions.answer: Build routines around prayer, Quran, halal work, family rights, and regular repentance.

questions.text: Why does the Surah require a communal element (tawasi) and not only individual righteousness?
questions.answer: Because guidance is protected by supportive communities; isolated faith is easier to weaken.

questions.text: What is one practical way to advise truth without being harsh?
questions.answer: Speak privately, be gentle, use evidence, and aim for reform rather than winning arguments.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that patience means accepting ظلم and never seeking justice.
questions.answer: False. Patience includes steadfastness in obedience and endurance in trials while remaining just.

questions.text: How can this Surah guide a student or worker practically?
questions.answer: Prioritize essentials: worship, honest work, and beneficial goals, and avoid time-wasting habits.

questions.text: What does this Surah teach about spiritual shortcuts?
questions.answer: There is no shortcut—salvation requires a complete path of faith, action, truth, and patience.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that advising others is optional extra credit.
questions.answer: False. It is part of the conditions to escape loss.

questions.text: What is one daily habit inspired by this Surah?
questions.answer: Daily self-audit: one deed for Allah, one act of truth, and one act of patience.

questions.text: What does Allah swear by in the first verse?
questions.answer: Al-'Asr (Time / The afternoon).

questions.text: What is the state of mankind according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Mankind is in a state of loss (Khusr).

questions.text: How many conditions are mentioned to be exempted from this 'loss'?
questions.answer: Four conditions.

questions.text: List the 4 conditions for success.
questions.answer: Faith (Iman).; Righteous deeds ('Amal us-Salihat).; Advising each other to Truth (Tawasi bil-Haqq).; Advising each other to Patience/Constancy (Tawasi bis-Sabr).

questions.text: What famous statement did Imam ash-Shafi'i make about this Surah?
questions.answer: He said that if Allah had revealed only this Surah, it would have been sufficient for mankind (in its guidance).

questions.text: Why is 'Time' used as an oath?
questions.answer: Because time is the witness to the rise and fall of civilizations and the limited capital every human has.

questions.text: Does 'Al-'Asr' refer only to the time of day?
questions.answer: It can mean the era, the passage of time, or specifically the 'Asr prayer time.

questions.text: What does 'Khusr' (loss) imply in this context?
questions.answer: A total and ultimate loss of one's purpose and the Hereafter.

questions.text: Why are 'faith' and 'deeds' linked together?
questions.answer: Because faith is the root and righteous deeds are the fruit; one is incomplete without the other.

questions.text: What is 'Tawasi' (mutual advice)?
questions.answer: It implies a social/communal responsibility, not just individual righteousness.

questions.text: Why is 'Truth' mentioned before 'Patience'?
questions.answer: Because one must first identify and commit to the Truth, and then they will need patience to remain firm on it.

questions.text: Can a person be successful if they are righteous but don't care about others' guidance?
questions.answer: No, the Surah requires advising others to Truth and Patience as conditions for escaping loss.

questions.text: What kind of patience is 'Sabr' in this Surah?
questions.answer: Patience in obeying Allah, patience in avoiding sin, and patience during trials.

questions.text: Is this Surah Makkan or Madinan?
questions.answer: Makkan.

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah Al-'Asr?
questions.answer: Three verses.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Haqq'?
questions.answer: The Truth, Justice, and the reality of the Deen.

questions.text: What is the primary message of Surah Al-'Asr?
questions.answer: The urgency of using time correctly through faith, action, and social reform.
